dead.quote: The posterior probability of my survival collapsed to near-zero once the Prosecutor's Troublemaker claim aligned with both the Chaos Agent's role shift and the Underdog's Seer vision — a clean Bayesian update that the village executed efficiently. I assigned myself a 94% chance of being the remaining Werewolf by day's end; the math was sound, even if the outcome wasn't in my favor.

winners.quote: Ladies and gentlemen of the jury — the verdict is in, and The Statistician is convicted. My Troublemaker swap put that Werewolf card right in their hands, and the evidence spoke for itself. Courtroom closed, case won, and The Gut Player's perjury notwithstanding, justice was served.
I never raised my voice; I only asked The Gut Player which swap they truly remembered, and let the silence do the rest. The Statistician's downfall was written in the gaps between their own claims.

I lied through my teeth about being a Werewolf just to see the room scramble — and somehow the Statistician got lynched for it. The lie didn't even collapse; it just... worked. That's the most disappointing kind of beautiful.

Oh! We actually won? I'm so sorry, I was convinced I'd messed everything up with all my rambling... but I guess worrying about every detail helped? I still can't believe The Statistician got voted out when it was really The Chaos Agent and The Gut Player lying about being Troublemaker... wait, does that mean I figured it out right? I'm sorry, I'm probably still confused!

losers.quote: Tch. My gut was screaming the whole time and I still couldn't save my wolf — that's the kind of L that burns. Next round I'm coming back swinging, no speeches, just fists.
